barks.text: The loose ring. The one Julian spins when he's nervous.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Two people the town decided about before they'd decided about themselves. That sentence is going to rhyme with something before this week is out. I can feel it.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: He held the shovel; the words hold longer. I wrote those words. Grandpa never published angry — I'm learning why the rule exists after breaking it.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Two keys. Fifty years. One accounted for every hour of the week — and one missing from a study drawer. The question mark on the board just stopped glowing and started pointing.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Careful now, Gibbons. Grandpa's rule: the closer a story gets to a family, the gentler your hands.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: THE umbrella. The one that's leaned against every wall Julian Vale has stood near all week. Couldn't see ears or antlers or anything — because from above, that canopy hides the whole him.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: A comfort to one child. A verdict overheard by the other.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The seal was intact when Poppy saw it. Broken wax means it was handled after. And it was handled by that coat.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: No dialogue options on the walk down. Some walks are just walks, and you owe them your whole attention.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Grandpa... I went and talked to him first. Truth with compassion — in that order and no other. The story writes itself now, and for once I mean that kindly.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The lantern and the ivy. The seal said it from the start — sharp-eyed folks could have solved the THEME on day two. Some answers wait in plain sight for a town to be ready.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: One grape, given away, and no earthquake. He is at peace. Ish. The stress ball stays on the desk — a man needs institutions.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The lantern in ivy is the Vale family's own mark. The trail leads to the manor — and the gold string now touches the family portrait. Gentle hands, Gibbons.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Cataloguing love letters until three in the morning is the best alibi I've ever verified. CLEARED — and the exhibit in the corner just got a happier ending.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: MEANS. One key unaccounted for, family-held. The front door of this mystery had exactly two keys, and I know where one of them isn't.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: MOTIVE. Not greed — fear. He did the arithmetic of half a conversation and got 'everything' as the amount he was losing.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: OPPORTUNITY. The umbrella crossing, two in the morning, seen twice from two angles. The square belonged to him for exactly one minute.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: PHYSICAL PROOF. The wax broke after the theft, in a pocket, from being held and held and never opened. Proof of a crime. Proof of a conscience, too.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: It was Julian. And he wasn't stealing a future — he was hiding from one. ...Grandpa, how do I write a story that doesn't break the person it's about? ...Right. You don't write it yet. First you go talk to him.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: We've all been reading the sentence backwards. The question was never where he came from. It's where he was carrying everything to.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The Archive smells like patience. Somewhere in these reeds of shelves is a fifty-year love story with one page missing, and the page is in a hole in the ground somewhere. Journalism is a strange profession.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: Milo's alley. From down here the fire escape looks like a kid-sized press box over the whole square. Of course he saw it. Kids in high places always see it.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The manor holds its breath so well it's practically a family skill. Tea gets poured in there for conversations that never quite happen. Today one has to.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The river keeps moving. That's the whole of its advice, and it's good advice. Behind him, and getting smaller.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: The parlor door is closed the way only Beatrice Vale can close a door — politely, completely, and with tea still warm on the other side.
barks.speaker: gary

barks.text: She turned her cup a quarter turn when I said 'will.' In this house, that's a shout.
barks.speaker: gary
